purpose: Test whether the bounded incomparable-kernel result survives a genuinely adversarial conceptual challenge to scope, preservation, equivalence, costs, candidate construction, invariance, ablation, minimality, and claim boundaries.

independence.required: A separate human or organization not involved in constructing the USD artifacts, with authority to redefine objections and produce countermodels within the frozen challenge rules.
eligible: formal-methods, logic, knowledge-representation, philosophy-of-science, or adjacent experts able to audit the complete package
teams permitted to argue that the registered scope, preservation contract, equivalence relation, cost accounting, or candidate universe is defective
challengers who disclose relevant prior exposure and conflicts
disqualified: authors or builders of the challenged artifacts
a nominally separate run controlled by the same person, organization, or agent workflow
challengers coached toward a preferred verdict
teams denied access to negative controls, failed cases, or unresolved gates

freeze_and_access.release_identifier: immutable Git commit SHA or signed archive digest
freeze_and_access.post_access_repairs: Any substantive repair after first access creates a new protocol/package version; the original challenge record remains immutable.
freeze_and_access.author_response_order: The adversarial team freezes its initial report before receiving an author rebuttal.

mandatory_attack_domains: primitive and derived machinery cost accounting
candidate-family construction and hidden winner bias
counterexamples, negative controls, and unresolved theorem gates
independence labels and universal-structure claim boundaries
required_outputs: at least one attempted countermodel or impossibility argument for each mandatory attack domain
explicit separation of blocking defects, scope limitations, nonblocking qualifications, and rejected attacks
a record of all author contact and all deviations from the frozen package

decision_rules.survived: Every mandatory attack domain is completed; no blocking defect or valid scope-limiting counterexample defeats the registered bounded result; nonblocking qualifications are preserved.
decision_rules.scope_limited: At least one valid adversarial result narrows a registered scope, preservation claim, invariance class, necessity claim, or minimal-frontier claim without refuting the entire bounded result.
decision_rules.refuted: A blocking defect, valid countermodel, invalid equivalence/cost rule, or reproducible contradiction defeats a load-bearing registered conclusion.
decision_rules.unresolved: Independence, package completeness, attack coverage, or evidence is insufficient for another verdict.
decision_rules.weakest_gate: The terminal verdict may not be stronger than the weakest completed mandatory attack domain.

nonclaims: the bounded result has survived external criticism
